footer.university: King Mongkut's Institute of Technology Ladkrabang

iot.a1: A technology that connects various devices and hardware to the internet, enabling data communication and automation. This allows for data analysis and practical application, such as in Smart Homes, Smart Farms, or Smart Factories.
iot.a2: Systems for processing various forms of data, starting from data collection via network devices (Databases), data processing, to data analysis. This maximizes utility and ensures network systems are efficient and secure (Cyber Security).
iot.q3: What is IoT and Information Engineering?
iot.a3: A program that integrates "IoT" (Hardware & Embedded Systems) with "Information Technology" (Data Management). It aims to produce engineers capable of designing and developing data-driven Intelligent Systems to meet the needs of Industry 4.0 & 5.0.
iot.whyDesc: The Bachelor of Engineering Program in IoT System and Information Engineering focuses on producing graduates as innovators who create technologies meeting national demands. The program integrates 4 core areas: Embedded Systems & Smart Devices, Software Development, Internet Networks, and Data Application & Management. Students are equipped to design effective industrial solutions, while promoting language and entrepreneurial skills to lead in the global workforce.
iot.learnTitle: What will you learn in IoTE?
iot.learnOverview: Overview: Focuses on 4 core pillars: Hardware (Devices/Circuits), Software (Programming/Apps), Network (Communication), and Data/AI (Information/Artificial Intelligence).
iot.y1Desc: Study basic Science & Math, Computer Programming, fundamental engineering skills, and an introduction to IoT and Information systems.
iot.y2Desc: Learn digital circuits, electronics, Computer Networks, Object-Oriented Programming (OOP), and engineering tools for system analysis and design.
iot.y3Desc: Focus on Embedded Systems, Database Management, Data Communication, Cyber Security, and advanced data analysis. Engage in hands-on IoT hardware and software projects.
iot.y4Desc: Complete a Capstone Project or Co-operative Education in real workplaces. Learn modern technological topics to step into a professional engineering career.

dual.title2: Faculty of Engineering & Faculty of Science, KMITL
dual.title3: B.Eng. in IoT System and Information Engineering & B.Sc. in Industrial Physics
dual.desc: The Dual Degree program addresses modern global demands by seamlessly producing personnel expert in both engineering and applied science (Physics). Students earn 2 bachelor's degrees, learning from physics fundamentals and hardware/software design, to practical industrial applications for future technologies like Smart Electronics, Smart Agriculture, Smart Factory, and Smart City.

ceiot.title1: B.Eng. Computer Engineering and IoT (Continuing Program)
ceiot.quote: “From High Vocational Certificate to Full-Fledged Engineer, Up-to-date with AI, IoT & Digital Tech”
ceiot.desc: This program is designed to upgrade High Vocational Certificate (Diplomas) holders into highly skilled engineers ready for Digital Transformation, emphasizing "Practicality" and "Industry Readiness".
ceiot.degList1: Full Name (Thai): วิศวกรรมศาสตรบัณฑิต สาขาวิศวกรรมคอมพิวเตอร์และไอโอที
ceiot.degList2: Full Name (English): Bachelor of Engineering Program in Computer Engineering and IoT
ceiot.degList3: Abbreviation (Thai): วศ.บ. วิศวกรรมคอมพิวเตอร์และไอโอที
ceiot.degList4: Abbreviation (English): B.Eng. Computer Engineering and IoT
ceiot.degList5: Total Credits: 118 Credits (3-Year Continuing Bachelor's Degree)
ceiot.careerList_2: Embedded System Engineer / Embedded Software Engineer
ceiot.plo1: PLO 1: Apply computer engineering knowledge in hardware, networks, software, and IoT technology to solve engineering problems appropriately.
ceiot.plo2: PLO 2: Design and develop systems or solutions in computer engineering and IoT that meet specific requirements, considering cyber security and system efficiency.
ceiot.plo3: PLO 3: Integrate computer engineering and IoT knowledge (from devices, communication, platforms to UI) to solve industrial problems effectively. Continuously adapt and learn new related technologies.
ceiot.plo4: PLO 4: Conduct projects to solve industrial problems appropriately through self-directed learning processes.
ceiot.plo5: PLO 5: Possess practical skills in selecting, utilizing, and testing engineering tools, equipment, software, and methods correctly.
ceiot.plo6: PLO 6: Work effectively with others as both leaders and followers. Communicate technically and present professional work appropriately in both Thai and English.
ceiot.plo7: PLO 7: Adhere to morality, ethics, and social and environmental responsibility in the computer engineering and IoT profession.

admissionTab.heading: ADMISSION (5 Seats)
admissionTab.cond2: - Requires TGAT, TPAT3, A-Level Math 1, and Physics scores

portProjects.p1_2: 1. Currently studying in Grade 12 (Science-Math) or 3rd-year Vocational Certificate in related fields (e.g., Mechanical, Electrical, Electronics).
portProjects.p1_3: 2. Minimum GPAX of 3.00 over at least 4 semesters.
portProjects.p1_4: 3. Must have won at least 1 competition from the specified list.
portProjects.p1_5: 4. Free from severe diseases that obstruct studying.
portProjects.p1_6: 5. Good conduct and willingness to strictly follow the institute's rules.

portProjects.p2_6: 3.2 Winner of the 'e-Waste HACK BKK' project (For IoT and Information Engineering applicants only).
portProjects.p2_7: 4. Free from severe diseases that obstruct studying.
portProjects.p2_8: 5. Good conduct and willingness to strictly follow the institute's rules.

department.title: Department
department.iote: Department of IoT and Information Engineering

cyber.desc1: is a dedicated environment where students, professionals, and researchers can practice and enhance their skills in defending against and analyzing cyber threats. These labs are equipped with tools and technologies that simulate real-world cyber attacks and defense strategies, allowing users to test their knowledge in a controlled, safe setting.
cyber.desc2: Cyber security labs typically focus on two main areas:
cyber.point1: 1. Defensive Security: This includes activities like setting up firewalls, intrusion detection systems, encryption methods, and network security configurations to protect against attacks.
cyber.point2: 2. Offensive Security: In this area, participants engage in ethical hacking and penetration testing to identify and exploit vulnerabilities in systems, websites, or networks, helping to build a deeper understanding of how attackers operate.
